response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and systems software engineer with 15+ years of experience, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and application-layer development. The Principal Performance Architect role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware-software co-design, and deep-learning workload optimization — none of which appear in his resume. His closest relevant experience is low-latency async I/O work at Dunnhumby and brief C/C++ component development at National Instruments, but these are insufficient proxies for the hardware architecture and ML systems competencies the role requires.
strengths: Demonstrated low-latency systems programming at scale (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, DLang) at Dunnhumby
Broad language experience including C/C++ in a measurement-engineering context at National Instruments
Strong software craftsmanship foundation (TDD, CI/CD, performance-aware design) transferable to any engineering discipline
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience of any kind; hardware architecture is entirely absent from the resume
No exposure to AI/ML workloads, deep neural network training or inference optimization, or GPU/accelerator architecture
Educational background (two bachelor's degrees, no master's or PhD) falls short of the preferred qualifications for a principal-level hardware/AI-systems role

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware architecture experience
No AI/DL workload or accelerator expertise
